vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1547: fix: @panctl/cli npm install warns on Node <22 (engine mismatch + deprecated deps)

plan.title: fix: @panctl/cli npm install warns on Node <22 (engine mismatch + deprecated deps)
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-8

narratives.Problem: Installing `@panctl/cli` emits noisy npm warnings. A real production install of the published 0.21.0 on Node 22.22.0 emits exactly four: (1) ini@7.0.0 EBADENGINE — from effect@4.0.0-beta.73 — which fires even on supported Node 22.0–22.22.1 because ini@7's engines are `^22.22.2 || ^24.15.0 || >=26.0.0`; (2) glob@10.5.0 'security vulnerabilities' deprecation — from @google-cloud/speech → google-gax → rimraf@5; (3) node-domexception@1.0.0 deprecation — from @google-cloud/speech → google-gax → node-fetch@3 → fetch-blob; (4) prebuild-install@7.1.3 'no longer maintained' — from node-pty + better-sqlite3 + @xenova/transformers→sharp. The @panctl/cli >=22.0.0 engine warning itself only appears on Node <22 and is correct/intentional.
narratives.Proposal: Fix at the root cause we actually control. EMPIRICALLY PROVEN: a package's own `overrides` are ignored for `npm install -g` (only the root project's overrides win), so overrides cannot help end users. Both heavyweight culprits are lazy-loaded dynamic imports with no static type references, so we make `@google-cloud/speech` and `@xenova/transformers` NOT-auto-installed optional dependencies (peerDependencies + peerDependenciesMeta.optional). Making @google-cloud/speech optional eliminates BOTH glob@10.5 (CVE notice) and node-domexception in one move; making @xenova/transformers optional removes sharp and lightens the install. Each dynamic-import site gets an actionable 'install X to use Y' error. We ACCEPT and DOCUMENT the two genuinely-upstream residuals: prebuild-install (node-pty + better-sqlite3 hard-require it; no replacement) and ini@7 EBADENGINE on Node <22.22.2 (effect's transitive). REJECTED: npm-shrinkwrap.json (disproportionate, fragile on a bun monorepo) and bumping the engines floor to >=22.22.2 (past the maintainer's own Node 22.22.0). README is updated to state the Node 22 requirement clearly (recommend latest 22.x), the new opt-in installs, and the expected residual notices. A verification bead re-runs the real install probe to prove glob + node-domexception are gone.

autoDecisions.summary: Fix glob@10.5 + node-domexception by making @google-cloud/speech a NOT-auto-installed optional (peer) dependency rather than via overrides.
autoDecisions.rationale: Overrides are proven ignored for `npm install -g`. @google-cloud/speech is the single direct dep that pulls BOTH transitives, is referenced only at one lazy dynamic-import site with no static type imports, and STT is a niche feature — so opt-in install is a defensible, reversible, root-cause fix we fully control.

Same shape (one lazy dynamic import, no static types). Removes sharp (and its deprecations) and lightens the default install. Local doc-embedding indexing is niche and degrades gracefully to an actionable error.

autoDecisions.summary: ACCEPT and DOCUMENT prebuild-install@7.1.3 as an unavoidable residual.
autoDecisions.rationale: node-pty and better-sqlite3 are core, both hard-require prebuild-install, and there is no non-deprecated replacement. Chasing it would mean forking core native deps — out of proportion to a transitive deprecation notice.

autoDecisions.summary: ACCEPT and DOCUMENT ini@7 EBADENGINE (only on Node <22.22.2); surface the Node 22 requirement in README instead of forcing it.
autoDecisions.rationale: Rejected npm-shrinkwrap.json (fragile maintenance burden on a bun-managed monorepo; npm-tree vs bun-tree divergence) and rejected raising engines to >=22.22.2 (past the maintainer's own Node 22.22.0, would warn on the maintainer's machine). Recommending latest Node 22.x in docs naturally avoids the warning for compliant users.

narrative.Action: Remove `@google-cloud/speech` from `dependencies` in the root package.json and declare it as an optional peer dependency (`peerDependencies` + `peerDependenciesMeta['@google-cloud/speech'].optional = true`), keeping it resolvable for development (devDependencies and/or bun install). Update the single dynamic-import site at src/voice/google-cloud-transcription.ts (~line 88) so that a missing module produces a clear, actionable error naming the exact install command (e.g. `npm install -g @google-cloud/speech`) instead of a raw 'Cannot find module'. This establishes the optional-peer-dep pattern that the transformers bead reuses and the verification bead checks. Verify with a real `npm install @panctl/cli --omit=dev` probe (in a temp dir) that glob@10.5.0 AND node-domexception are no longer in the resolved tree, and that `npm run typecheck && npm run build && npm test` still pass in the dev workspace.

subItems.title: @google-cloud/speech is removed from `dependencies` and declared as an optional peer dep; it is NOT present in a fresh `npm install @panctl/cli --omit=dev` resolved tree.
A real production install probe shows glob@10.5.0 and node-domexception@1.0.0 deprecation warnings are GONE.
When @google-cloud/speech is absent at runtime, the Google Cloud transcription path emits an actionable error naming the install command — not a raw module-not-found.
`npm run typecheck`, `npm run build`, and `npm test` pass in the dev workspace (package still resolvable for development).

narrative.Action: Apply the same optional-peer-dep pattern established by `speech-optional` to `@xenova/transformers`: remove it from `dependencies`, declare it optional-peer, keep it resolvable for development, and make the single dynamic-import site at src/lib/docs/index-builder.ts (~line 417) emit an actionable error when absent (naming the install command). This removes sharp (and sharp's own deprecations) from the default install. NOTE: it does NOT remove prebuild-install — node-pty and better-sqlite3 still require it; that residual is handled by the docs bead.

subItems.title: @xenova/transformers is removed from `dependencies` and declared as an optional peer dep; it is NOT present in a fresh `npm install @panctl/cli --omit=dev` resolved tree.
A real production install probe shows sharp (and its deprecations) no longer pulled via @xenova/transformers.
When @xenova/transformers is absent at runtime, local-embedding doc indexing emits an actionable error naming the install command.
`npm run typecheck`, `npm run build`, and `npm test` pass in the dev workspace.

items.title: Document Node 22 requirement, opt-in optional features, and expected residual deprecations

narrative.Action: Update README install/prerequisites: state the Node 22 requirement clearly and recommend the latest Node 22.x (>=22.22.2) to avoid the upstream effect→ini@7 engine notice; document that Google Cloud STT (`@google-cloud/speech`) and local doc-embedding indexing (`@xenova/transformers`) are now opt-in installs, with the exact commands; and add a short 'known install notices' note explaining the residual `prebuild-install` deprecation (transitive of node-pty/better-sqlite3, expected, harmless) and the ini@7 engine notice on Node <22.22.2. Docs only — no code.

subItems.title: README states Node 22 is required and recommends the latest 22.x (>=22.22.2) to avoid the upstream ini engine notice.
README documents that @google-cloud/speech and @xenova/transformers are opt-in installs, with the exact install commands.
README includes a brief note that the residual prebuild-install deprecation (and ini@7 notice on Node <22.22.2) are expected upstream transitive notices.

items.title: Verify and capture the reduced install warning set

narrative.Action: After speech-optional and transformers-optional land, run the real install probe used during planning — a clean `npm install @panctl/cli --omit=dev` from a temp project (or `npm pack` of the local build installed into a temp dir to validate pre-publish) — and capture the before/after warning sets. Assert glob@10.5.0 and node-domexception@1.0.0 are GONE, and that only the documented residuals (ini@7 on Node <22.22.2; prebuild-install) remain. Record the captured output in the PR description / issue as the evidence the fix meets the issue's 'eliminate transitive noise' bar.

subItems.title: A captured real-install probe shows glob@10.5.0 and node-domexception@1.0.0 warnings absent.
The captured output shows only the documented residuals (prebuild-install; ini@7 on Node <22.22.2) and is attached to the PR/issue as evidence.
